prompt: BACKGROUND: Photorealistic deep space — deep navy to black, heavy ominous purple-pink nebula clouds at upper half, cold white star field at lower half, tense and precarious atmosphere. FOREGROUND: Flat 2D cartoon — at center frame, a large bold-outlined white cartoon razor blade or knife edge shape (~30% frame width) balanced horizontally at center. On top of the razor edge, a teal W boson sphere and a violet Z boson sphere (~7% frame width each) balance precariously. Bold red tipping arrows on each side of the blade suggest the universe could tip either way. The razor edge is impossibly thin, balancing the bosons against the void of space.

segment: #6 W And Z Bosons, Predicted 1967-1968, Discovered 1983.
scene_text: These particles are absurdly heavy. The Z boson is about 100 times heavier than a proton.

The extreme mass comparison is the entire visual: one giant violet sphere vs. one hundred tiny proton clusters.
